_instructions: Brand positioning, offer context, and identity signals. Read by brand-voice, lead-magnet, case-study, testimonial-extractor, and launch-content-planner. Gives those skills the business context they need beyond voice alone.

brand_identity.tagline: [OPTIONAL — a short phrase that captures your positioning]
brand_identity.what_you_do: [ONE SENTENCE — your offer or service, plain language]
brand_identity.who_you_serve: [SPECIFIC AUDIENCE — mirror audience.json primary but from your POV]
brand_identity.primary_outcome_you_deliver: [THE RESULT A CLIENT/CUSTOMER GETS — specific and measurable where possible]

offers.name: [OFFER NAME 1]
offers.cta: [THE SPECIFIC CTA FOR THIS OFFER — e.g. 'DM me SYSTEM' or 'Book a call at URL']

brand_identity.content_mission: [ONE SENTENCE — who the content is for, what it does for them, how it's different. Format: 'We create [format] for [audience] who [situation], so they can [outcome] — without [common alternative that doesn't work].']

positioning.category: [WHAT CATEGORY YOU COMPETE IN — e.g. 'B2B content strategy' OR 'AI writing tools' OR 'executive coaching']
positioning.differentiation: [WHAT MAKES YOU DIFFERENT FROM OTHERS IN THIS CATEGORY]
positioning.against: [WHAT COMMON APPROACH, ADVICE, OR COMPETITOR YOU POSITION AGAINST — not as an attack, but as context]

notable_results: [RESULT 1 — specific and real, e.g. '4,200 newsletter subscribers in 90 days for a solo founder']
notable_clients_or_logos: [CLIENT/COMPANY 1 — use 'a Series A SaaS founder' style if you prefer not to name]
